tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: What type of micrograph is this?

dialogs.thought: Check for any labels/scale bars or modality annotations that might indicate the micrograph type.
Identify the imaging modality by overall appearance (contrast, ultrastructural detail) and context (inset, arrows, scale bar).

content.content: Grayscale high-magnification micrograph showing ultrastructural cellular detail with organelle-like dense bodies, layered membrane-like structures, arrows pointing to small features, and an inset magnified region; includes scale bars typical of electron microscopy.

arguments.attribute: ultrastructural features and image characteristics (e.g., membranes/organelles) suggesting modality

dialogs.thought: Confirm ultrastructural (electron microscopy) features in the inset region to support modality classification.

content.content: Inset region shows finely granular cytoplasm with multiple electron-dense round bodies and sharp membrane boundaries at nanometer-scale detail, consistent with transmission electron microscopy appearance.

dialogs.content: This is an electron micrograph, which is an image captured using an electron microscope. Electron microscopes use a beam of electrons instead of light to magnify the sample, allowing for much higher resolution and magnification than traditional light microscopes.
